message:
fix: queryReferencedColumns under-fetches when computedColumns present

Computed column functions are opaque JS closures — we can't statically
analyze which source columns they reference. When computedColumns is
non-empty, fall back to reading all columns so the functions have
access to every field they may need. Without this, `.select("name",
"total").compute("total", row => row.price * row.qty)` would not read
`price` or `qty`, causing the function to receive `undefined`.
